Natural Language Processing

3  |
Type:
UEM31  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Tutorial:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Natural Language Processing (NLP) aims to develop tools and methods capable of apprehending the semantics of natural language to facilitate knowledge acquisition and exploitation. The objective of this module is to present the problems posed for NLP and the main models for analysing, synthesising, exploiting, and producing documents.
Course Content (21h)
Chapter I – Introduction to NLP
Brief history and evolution of the field
Major NLP challenges: ambiguity, implicit meaning, linguistic variability
Application domains and current issues
Chapter II – Linguistic Processing Levels
Presentation of different levels: from low-level to semantic processing
Importance of modular processing structure
Chapter III – Lexical Level
Objectives of lexical processing
Lexical access: dictionaries, lemmatisation, lexemes
Introduction to morphology: inflection, derivation, affixation
Chapter IV – Syntactic Level
Syntax notions and formal grammars
Syntactic constituents and sentence structures
Paraphrases and syntactic reductions
Syntax trees and bottom-up/top-down analysis
Typical problems in syntactic processing
Chapter V – Semantic Level
Conceptual representations of meaning
Limitations of functional representations (frames, first-order logic)
Syntax-semantics interface: meaning carriers, thematic roles
Chapter VI – Pragmatic Level
Intention, context, speech acts
Contextual interpretation and implicit meaning management
Coherence and pragmatic inference
Chapter VII – NLP Applications
Document processing: indexing, summarisation, classification
Automatic document production: text generation, synthesis
Natural interfaces: conversational agents, chatbots, question-answering
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — written tests, personal work
Bibliography
Daniel Jurafsky & James H. Martin (2000).
Speech and Language Processing: An Introduction to Natural Language Processing, Computational Linguistics and Speech Recognition
. 1st Edition. ISBN-10: 0130950696.
